title: woof project prerequisites
description: Project-side declaration at .woof/prerequisites.toml. Tells woof what infrastructure, public model CLIs, languages, and indexing tools the project depends on. The tool-side language registry (woof/languages/<lang>.toml) provides the install instructions per language. woof preflight verifies everything declared here. Two-tier caching: floor checks (binaries/versions/grammars/LSP plugins) cached 24h, hashed on this file plus referenced language registry tomls.

infra.description: Required infrastructure binaries for this consumer repository. Keys are binary names (git, just, docker, gh, ...); values are version specifications. Floor checks verify presence and minimum version. Declare gh only for hosted trackers or project commands that need it.

commands.description: Required public model CLIs used by woof dispatch. Woof must construct raw claude and codex invocations itself and must not require private cld/cod wrappers or external sync tools.

validators.description: Required schema validators. ajv and ajv-formats are mandatory: woof validate uses ajv-cli for all internal artefact validation, three schemas (gate, critique, jsonl-events) use the format keyword which ajv-cli only enforces when ajv-formats is loaded via -c ajv-formats, and Stage 5 Check 4 calls `ajv compile` for every owned json_schema_ref. schemathesis is an optional declaration for consumer projects that drive their own OpenAPI conformance through a quality-gate command; Stage 5 Check 4 itself does not run schemathesis.

tracker.description: Issue-tracker declaration. Woof keeps the epic-level contract in the declared tracker and the per-epic runtime under .woof/epics/E<N>/. See ADR-003.

kind.description: Issue-tracker adapter. 'github' keeps each epic's contract in a GitHub issue (one issue per epic, E<N> equals the GitHub issue number, Woof verifies reachability when the graph resumes tracker-backed state). 'local' keeps the contract entirely under .woof/epics/E<N>/ with no remote, so any repository can run Woof without a hosted tracker.

repo.description: GitHub repository as <owner>/<name>. Required when kind = 'github'; ignored for other kinds.

grammars.description: Grammar names matching woof/languages/<name>.toml entries. Preflight parses each language's verify_snippet under verify_scope to confirm the grammar works.

languages.description: Languages requiring LSP. Preflight resolves each via woof/languages/<lang>.toml and verifies binary + plugin presence.

host.description: Project-specific host prerequisites that cannot be represented as simple versioned binaries. Preflight checks the current platform and runs declared commands from the project root.

platforms.description: Supported host platforms for this consumer checkout.

checks.description: Named project-specific host checks. Each command must exit 0 when the host is ready.

servers.description: Named local or project-specific server prerequisites. Each entry either runs a readiness command from the project root or probes a URL for the expected HTTP status.

cartography.description: Cartography contract (ADR-004). `woof preflight` requires this block and enforces the cartography artefact group at .woof/codebase/: the consumer-owned scripts/refresh-cartography script (present and executable), the human-authored design docs (TARGET-ARCHITECTURE.md and PRINCIPLES.md, present and not stubs), and the mechanical layer (tags, files.txt, freshness.json, present). `woof init` scaffolds this block so new consumers are enforced by default; older consumers without it must re-run the /woof setup and map-codebase onboarding path.

staleness_floor_hours.description: Age in hours beyond which a present freshness.json is reported as stale. Staleness is a non-blocking warning with a refresh prompt, never a preflight failure. The presence of freshness.json is part of the blocking mechanical-layer check; only its age is governed by this floor.

summary_min_chars.description: Minimum body length in characters (excluding YAML front matter) for a design doc to count as authored. A shorter doc fails preflight as a stub unless its front matter marks it intentionally complete (status: complete, or complete: true).

languages.description: Languages whose per-language refresh-cartography fragments (woof/languages/<lang>.toml) compose the consumer's scripts/refresh-cartography. Drives `woof init` script composition; each name must match a language-registry entry.

stub_marker.description: Marker string that setup templates embed in design docs. A design doc that still contains this marker fails preflight as an unedited stub; removing the marker (and authoring real content) is what makes preflight pass.

version_spec.description: Version specification. 'any' = presence-only check. 'X', 'X.Y', 'X.Y.Z' = exact-floor (preflight accepts equal-or-greater). Trailing '+' is permitted as documentation; the floor semantics apply either way.

command.description: Command run from the project root. It must exit 0 when the prerequisite is satisfied.

required.description: Human-readable requirement shown in failed preflight output.

install.description: Command or concise instruction shown in failed preflight output.

command.description: Readiness command run from the project root. It must exit 0 when the server is ready.

url.description: HTTP URL to probe for readiness.

required.description: Human-readable requirement shown in failed preflight output.

install.description: Command or concise instruction shown in failed preflight output.
